_patch_meta.description: Reg 145 (sexuality) — reclassify from PARTIAL to Session D pointer. Notes updated with decision rationale. No status field changes.

set.notes: SESSION D POINTER — Researcher decision 2026-04-06. Reclassified from PARTIAL. Sexuality as a modern category has no single biblical lexical anchor (inference_note confirmed at Session A). The positive, created dimension of human sexuality is distributed across existing registries: flesh (#185, basar — embodied creatureliness), desire (#43, somatic dimension), covenant (#34), love (#103), and union/faithfulness vocabulary. The inner-being significance of sexual identity as a gendered being before God is a cross-registry synthesis question, not a single-term analysis question. No Session A run required. Session D synthesis should address: how the programme's data across flesh, desire, covenant, love, and unity together articulate the inner-being reality of sexuality as Scripture presents it. Source: Session-A-v9-Empty-Words-Analysis-20260318 (original); researcher decision 2026-04-06.

operations.description: Reg 145 (sexuality) notes updated — PARTIAL closed, Session D pointer recorded
